summary: Real-time event-tracking backend built with NestJS, Drizzle ORM (PostgreSQL), Redis, MQTT (EMQX), and Socket.IO. Solid architectural foundation with critical security and correctness issues to resolve before production.

issues.title: serial() misused as Foreign Key
issues.description: All FK columns (roleId, userId, eventId) use serial() instead of integer(). serial() adds a NOT NULL auto-increment default sequence that conflicts with actual FK values.
issues.fix: Replace all FK serial() columns with integer(). Affected: users.roleId, locationLogs.userId, eventStaff.eventId, eventStaff.userId, eventParticipants.eventId, eventParticipants.userId, anomalies.eventId, anomalies.userId.

issues.title: Hardcoded JWT secret fallback
issues.description: If JWT_SECRET env var is not set, the app falls back to 'default_secret', making all tokens trivially forgeable.
issues.fix: Use configService.getOrThrow<string>('JWT_SECRET') in both auth.module.ts and jwt.strategy.ts.

issues.title: Google Login is a security bypass stub
issues.description: googleLogin() only rejects the literal string 'invalid_token'. Any other token string grants auth as a hardcoded user with a dummy JWT.
issues.fix: Implement proper Google OAuth via google-auth-library, or remove the endpoint until it is ready.

issues.title: Missing global ValidationPipe
issues.description: class-validator decorators on DTOs (@IsEmail, @MinLength, etc.) are completely inert without a ValidationPipe. All DTO validation is currently non-functional.
issues.fix: Add app.useGlobalPipes(new ValidationPipe({ whitelist: true, forbidNonWhitelisted: true })) in bootstrap().

issues.title: No WebSocket authentication
issues.description: Any client can join any event room and receive live location data. There is no JWT validation on WS connection or on joinEventRoom.
issues.fix: Implement a WsGuard that validates JWT from socket handshake auth header and enforces event access authorization.

issues.title: Pervasive any types
issues.description: user: any, role: any, payload: any are used throughout the codebase, entirely bypassing TypeScript's type safety.
issues.fix: Define an AuthenticatedUser interface (id, email, role, permissions) and use it consistently across services and controllers.

issues.title: Race condition in joinEvent count
issues.description: currentCount is read and incremented as event.currentCount + 1 in application code. Concurrent joins can over-fill the event.
issues.fix: Use SQL-level atomic increment with a WHERE clause enforcing capacity: SET current_count = current_count + 1 WHERE current_count < max_participants.

issues.title: Broken import path (../../../../../src/)
issues.description: Imports traverse up to root and re-enter src/, creating a path that resolves to src/src/. Other files use ../../db/ correctly.
issues.fix: Change '../../../../../src/db/database.module' to '../../../../db/database.module'.

issues.title: Redundant DB query in login() — N+1
issues.description: login() queries users with 'with: { role: true }' but then performs a separate roles.findFirst() query anyway. The comment incorrectly claims the relation is unavailable.
issues.fix: Remove the separate role query; use user.role from the joined result.

issues.title: Weak token entropy (Math.random())
issues.description: Math.random() is not cryptographically secure and produces only ~40 bits of entropy for the 8-character join token.
issues.fix: Use crypto.randomBytes(6).toString('hex').toUpperCase() for a cryptographically secure token.

issues.title: STUCK anomaly check compares position to itself
issues.description: getPreviousPosition() is called after updateParticipantPosition() in MqttService. Redis already holds the new position, so distance is always 0 and the check is broken.
issues.fix: Fetch previous position from Redis before calling updateParticipantPosition(), then pass it into detectAnomalies().

issues.title: Anomaly flooding — no deduplication
issues.description: Every MQTT message can trigger an insertAnomaly() write. A single prolonged anomaly generates dozens of identical DB rows.
issues.fix: Use a Redis key with TTL per (userId, eventId, anomalyType) as a cooldown gate before inserting.

issues.title: No CORS config or HTTP security headers
issues.description: The HTTP server exposes no CORS policy and no security headers (Helmet). WebSocket CORS is open (*) which is acceptable only in development.
issues.fix: Add app.use(helmet()) and app.enableCors({ origin: process.env.ALLOWED_ORIGINS }) in bootstrap().

issues.title: Orphaned locationLogs and anomalies on event delete
issues.description: deleteEvent() cleans up eventStaff and eventParticipants but leaves locationLogs and anomalies orphaned.
issues.fix: Add deletion of related records or use ON DELETE CASCADE on FK definitions.

issues.title: STAFF can delete events
issues.description: The DELETE /:id endpoint grants access to both SUPER_ADMIN and STAFF. Staff deleting events is almost certainly unintended.
issues.fix: Restrict @Roles to 'SUPER_ADMIN' only for the delete endpoint.

issues.title: Redis connection errors unhandled
issues.description: No error listener is registered on the ioredis client. A Redis failure causes silent crashes in the tracking pipeline.
issues.fix: Add this.redisClient.on('error', (err) => this.logger.error('Redis error', err)).

issues.title: Default battery value '100' is misleading
issues.description: When battery data is absent, '100' is stored, implying the device is fully charged.
issues.fix: Store 'unknown' or omit the field when battery data is not present.

issues.title: lat/lng stored as varchar instead of doublePrecision
issues.description: Geographic coordinates stored as strings misses proper indexing, range comparisons, and wastes storage.
issues.fix: Use doublePrecision() from drizzle-orm/pg-core for latitude and longitude columns.

issues.title: Event status as varchar instead of pgEnum
issues.description: The status column allows any string up to 50 chars. Database-level validation is absent.
issues.fix: Define export const eventStatusEnum = pgEnum('event_status', ['IDLE', 'LIVE', 'FINISHED']) and use it.

issues.title: Missing @IsOptional() on description field
issues.description: description has @IsString() but no @IsOptional(). Once ValidationPipe is added, this field becomes required even though the DB column is nullable.
issues.fix: Add @IsOptional() decorator before @IsString() on the description field.

issues.title: Unused Reflector injection
issues.description: Reflector is injected in JwtAuthGuard but never used in the guard body, suppressed only by an eslint-disable comment.
issues.fix: Remove the Reflector injection or implement IsPublic() skip logic that was likely intended.

issues.title: Unused @nestjs/microservices dependency
issues.description: @nestjs/microservices is listed as a production dependency but no microservices transport is used.
issues.fix: Remove @nestjs/microservices from package.json.

issues.title: No database migration workflow documented
issues.description: drizzle-kit is installed but there are no migration scripts or documentation. drizzle-kit push works only in dev; production needs generate + migrate.
issues.fix: Add drizzle:generate and drizzle:migrate scripts to package.json and document the workflow in README.md.

issues.title: locationLogs missing eventId FK
issues.description: locationLogs only stores userId. Filtering historical logs by event requires joining through eventParticipants.
issues.fix: Add an eventId integer FK to the locationLogs table.
